spec_match.rationale: Provides a mock injector showing allowed, reported, and blocked behaviors across different CSP-like headers.

interactive.rationale: Allows users to toggle postures (no policy, report-only, enforce) and click 'inject' buttons to observe counts and live log updates.

blurb_alignment.rationale: Perfectly demonstrates the rollout workflow (report-only → enforce) advocated by the specification.

edge_cases.rationale: Models various script profiles including good CDNs, legacy trackers, ads, and supply-chain threat vectors.

references.rationale: Correctly links to the canonical ChromeStatus feature entry `5178394056327168`.

design_system.rationale: Clean layout leveraging colored banners, card grids, and consistent CSS variable integration.

openQuestions.title: Simulate real reporting API payloads
openQuestions.detail: While the UI tracks reported count and visual status, it does not show the raw JSON structure that gets POSTed to the reporting endpoint. Adding a collapsible JSON payload preview for each reported row would enhance educational value.

summary: A superb concept page demonstrating transition rollouts from report-only to full enforcement using clean visual feed feedback and comprehensive target variations.
